Class WorkoutFormEvent

java.lang.Object
java.util.EventObject
hr.unizd.workoutlog.gui.WorkoutFormEvent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class WorkoutFormEvent extends EventObject
Carries the data the user entered in the input form when the "Add Workout" button is pressed.

This is the WorkoutLog equivalent of the classic form-event objects used in Swing MVC examples (such as LeftFormEvent / RightFormEvent). The input panel reads its own components, packs the values into one of these objects, and hands it to its listener. That way the panel never has to expose its internal components to the outside world (encapsulation).

It extends EventObject so the data is tied to the source component (the add button) that triggered the event.

Note: this is a transport object, not a domain class. It performs no validation — the values are passed on exactly as read from the form, and the domain rules are enforced later by Workout.

See Also:

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private boolean
    cardio
    Whether the "Cardio" box was checked.

    Exercise name exactly as typed by the user.
    private Intensity
    intensity
    Intensity level chosen with the radio buttons.
    private String
    note
    Optional note text.
    private boolean
    strength
